title: corydalis flexuosa subsp. flexuosa
intro: Corydalis flexuosa subsp. flexuosa, also known as Himalayan corydalis, is a perennial plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to the Himalayan region. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese and Ayurvedic medicine, where it is valued for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Corydalis flexuosa subsp. flexuosa include relief from pain, inflammation, and muscle spasms, as well as potential benefits for anxiety and insomnia. Corydalis flexuosa subsp. flexuosa is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and infusions, and is also available as a dried root or rhizome for use in tea or as a supplement.
